strengths: 10 well-differentiated pages aligned with seed taxonomy
Concrete interactions: faceted publication search, date scrubber tying map to table, cite-this dropdown, sparkline previews
Strong domain motifs (bathymetric contours, tide-table grids, hairline rules)
Clear content model with cruise IDs, ORCID, GeoJSON tracks, accession IDs
Live indicators strip (SST anomaly, ENSO, sea ice) signals genuine institutional dashboard
weaknesses: Some pages lean on generic 'directory grid' patterns without unique twist
Visual treatment slightly conservative; less sensory differentiation than candidate 2
No dedicated person-detail page despite PI focus

strengths: 10 pages with strong structural variety including a dedicated person-detail page
Distinctive motif: sonar two-line section headers, rust live-data dots, italic 'Fig.' captions
Rich domain-specific interactions: spectrogram audio player for bioacoustics, preview drawer with quick plots, live buoy strip with last-updated timestamp
Content model adds Sensor Station with last reading timestamp — reinforces continuous observation message
Rust accent reserved for live data creates legible information hierarchy
Discipline bands on /research feel editorial rather than templatey
weaknesses: Publications page slightly less detailed than candidate 1's right-rail saved-search treatment
Mobile sidebar slide-in described but discipline group behavior could be more specific
